mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding aggregate supply and stronger economic activity, leading markets to anticipate higher future short-term rates and inflation, which pushes the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 20-day Rank IC between INDPRO change and yield change is below 0.03 over any 2-year window, or the proportion of positive yield movements following INDPRO increases is not significantly above 50% (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A jump in the unemployment rate above the 5% threshold triggers a flight-to-safety into Treasuries as investors anticipate a weakening economy and possible Fed easing, compressing the 10-year yield downward over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In >40% of triggered instances, the 10-year yield rises (opposite direction) within 5 trading days, or the average yield change in triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation exceeds the federal funds rate, real rates become negative, signaling highly accommodative monetary policy that raises inflation expectations and term premiums, pushing the 10-year yield higher over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: In instances where CPI YoY > FEDFUNDS, the 60-day yield change is not positive in >60% of observations, or Spearman correlation between the real rate gap and subsequent yield change is insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During an expansion phase (GDP growth above trend), a further acceleration in real GDP signals overheating and prompts markets to price in tighter monetary policy, raising the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days. In a recession, the same GDP acceleration would have the opposite effect (yields fall), but this hypothesis is conditional on the expansion regime.
failure_condition: In expansion regimes, the 20-day yield change following a GDP growth above 2.5% is negative in >50% of cases, or the signal's IC in expansion vs. recession does not differ significantly (Mann-Whitney U test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation has a persistent upward trend that erodes bond purchasing power; as inflation rises, investors demand higher nominal yields to compensate, so the 10-year Treasury yield trends upward with inflation over any horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between CPIAUCSL monthly change and 20-day yield change is below 0.10, or the yield fails to rise in >55% of 20-day windows following positive CPIAUCSL prints.

mechanism_narrative: A large month-over-month decline in retail sales signals a sudden consumer spending contraction, triggering risk aversion and a flight to safe assets, which lowers the 10-year Treasury yield over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: In at least 40% of triggered 5-day windows, the 10-year yield rises (contradicts hypothesis), or the average yield change is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in both nonfarm payrolls (labor market) and housing starts (demand for residential construction) indicates broad-based economic momentum, which intensifies expectations of monetary tightening and pushes the 10-year yield higher over a 60-day horizon. The joint condition is necessary because either series alone could be noisy.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS and HOUST above their respective 3-month moving averages
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint signal over 60-day forward yield changes is below 0.03 over any 3-year period, or the average yield change in triggered periods is not significantly higher than in non-triggered periods (Welch t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle (defined by a 25bp or more lift in the fed funds rate over the past 6 months), further increases in FEDFUNDS lead to higher long-term yields as markets expect continued policy tightening and upward pressure on term premiums. This directional relationship is specific to tightening regimes; in easing cycles the opposite would hold.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has increased by at least 25bp over the past 6 months
failure_condition: Within tightening-cycle triggers, the 20-day yield change is negative in >50% of instances, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS change and subsequent yield change is non-positive (Spearman rho <= 0) with p > 0.10.
